115351.explanation: HattemCentraal cares about the (social) skills and expertise of the new council.

115352.explanation: According to HattemCentraal, this can reduce the distance between politics and society, making the municipality service-oriented towards its residents.

115355.explanation: This depends on the location, but in general, HattemCentraal provides space for local entrepreneurship.

115356.explanation: HattemCentraal wants everyone to be able to inquire locally about energy-saving options.

115357.explanation: This is an issue nationwide, but not here in Hattem. HattemCentraal is a local party focusing on local challenges and problems.

115358.explanation: According to HattemCentraal, this has too great an impact on our small-scale community.

115360.explanation: HattemCentraal is against a burning ban, but supports responsible burning.

115362.explanation: After the national fireworks ban, a local fireworks show is a necessary alternative. It can (possibly) help reduce the use of illegal and dangerous fireworks.

115363.explanation: In the longer term, HattemCentraal wants to establish a recycling center again for Hattem and collect bulky waste to prevent untidiness.

115367.explanation: For HattemCentraal, it is important that sports clubs, schools, and social organizations collaborate closely to encourage every (future) generation to be as active as possible.

115368.explanation: HattemCentraal wants to support clubs with advice and knowledge about, for example, legislation, finances, or organization. Initially, we stand for support with non-financial means, but if necessary, we are willing to consider providing guarantees.

115373.explanation: HattemCentraal wants BOA's (public order officers) not only to enforce rules but also to work with residents and neighborhood teams to prevent minor nuisances. They are the eyes and ears in the neighborhood.

115374.explanation: To keep Hattem safe, HattemCentraal focuses on (mobile) camera surveillance at risky and vulnerable places such as the Verkentoren, the Bleek, and the city center.

115376.explanation: A starter loan actually worsens the problem by driving up prices. According to HattemCentraal, flow in the housing market is very important. Normally, elderly people who want to downsize leave their family homes to starters.

115379.explanation: Permanent residence may bring risks due to differences in building regulations and remote location. It could be a breeding ground for undermining crime.

115380.explanation: New neighborhoods will probably have to be built in Hattem. HattemCentraal finds it very important that the character of the Hattem community does not change due to large-scale housing construction. It is therefore essential to carefully consider which new locations may be suitable for housing development.

115381.explanation: Only according to HattemCentraal should someone's abilities, health, and personal situation be taken into account (customized approach).

115383.explanation: HattemCentraal sees no reason to implement a change. With 120% of the social minimum, a larger group of households qualify for municipal support such as extraordinary assistance or Stichting Leergeld.

115385.explanation: Electric cars are an environmentally friendly way to travel. In consultation with residents, HattemCentraal wants to enable sufficient charging points nearby without designating specific locations.

115386.explanation: Drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists are all welcome in Hattem and should be well coordinated. According to HattemCentraal, the modified roads have not become clearer in recent years.

115387.explanation: That will not always be possible, because 'Care is scarce.' There is a growing shortage of resources (staff, time, money) in healthcare, while demand (due to aging, chronic diseases) increases, leading to waiting lists, high workloads, and difficult choices about who receives which care.

115388.explanation: Due to this group's vulnerability, HattemCentraal mainly focuses on quality and having one point of contact who together seeks the best future solution.

115389.explanation: HattemCentraal does not want to spend more than is earned. Property tax (OZB) and tourist tax should not, according to HattemCentraal, be used as a balancing item for deficits. Municipal debt must be reduced for future generations.

115392.explanation: Furthermore, HattemCentraal wants social rental housing for status holders to be allocated considering distribution across the city to accelerate integration.

115396.explanation: According to HattemCentraal, that is the responsibility of the primary schools themselves.

115399.explanation: HattemCentraal finds it very important that the character of the Hattem community does not change due to large-scale housing construction.

115400.explanation: A realistic contribution to the housing task can also be made, according to HattemCentraal, by allowing more flexibility in splitting homes/plots.

115401.explanation: No wind turbines and large solar fields for HattemCentraal.

115402.explanation: To meet current requirements, too large an investment is needed. HattemCentraal wants to keep civil affairs, some meeting places, and the wedding hall in the center. If workplaces are relocated, the back area can be used for something else.

115351.explanation: We go for appointments based on quality.

115352.explanation: Good governance is reliable, approachable, and close. In Hattem, we continue to strive for a municipal government that listens to residents, is clear in decision-making, and is open to cooperation. A referendum is a means, not a goal.

115355.explanation: This already happens a lot, for example on the Hoenwaard and at the Hilsdijk.

115356.explanation: We support local initiatives. The energy cooperative Powered by Hattem is an important partner in this. A business case for an energy project must essentially be feasible on its own. Referring to national schemes/subsidies remains a logical role for the municipality.

115357.explanation: There is no more space at H2O for such large-scale business activity in Hattem. Larger regional industrial areas such as Hessenpoort in Zwolle are suitable for this.

115358.explanation: We do not leave refugees and status holders to fend for themselves but help them as best as possible with housing and proper guidance to participate in Hattem. If Hattem is called upon, we will also take responsibility for the arrival of a reception center.

115360.explanation: We consider it important that proper information is provided to those who use wood stoves. For this reason, we support the objectives of the Clean Air Agreement.

115362.explanation: We encourage fireworks shows to be organized by and for Hattem residents, in coordination with the municipality.

115363.explanation: We think it is important that ROVA's service to collect bulky waste remains available, though against a reasonable fee.

115367.explanation: We consider it important that the Activity Contribution is maintained.

115368.explanation: RTV Hattem receives support from the municipality. We want this to continue, as it makes broadcasting the Council meetings possible.

115373.explanation: A safe city starts with the feeling of safety. That means we tackle nuisance, apply targeted enforcement, and improve places perceived as unsafe. Possible expansion is an option if needed.

115374.explanation: We are considering camera surveillance at locations like the Bleek due to the nuisance experienced there.

115375.explanation: We want to focus on a regional approach in cooperation with larger municipalities.

115376.explanation: It is a good way to help starters get their first home. However, there are financial risks for the municipality that need to be properly mapped out.

115379.explanation: We are pleased with the healthy and vital holiday parks in Hattem that can accommodate our tourists. We want to preserve them.

115380.explanation: First of all, Hattem is not a village but a city! We prefer infill development within existing urban areas, such as ’t Veen, the residential care zones, and Parkstaete, but we do not close our eyes to the necessity of expansion in the long term. We seek the right balance with respect to nature conservation.

115381.explanation: A social assistance benefit is desperately needed by many people and they must be able to rely on the municipality. According to ChristenUnie, this remains unconditional.

115383.explanation: The income limit varies by scheme. For each scheme, it must be reviewed whether this is sufficient for Hattem residents who need to use it.

115385.explanation: The use of electric cars has increased significantly in recent years. In 2023, more new electric cars were sold than fossil-fuel cars. Not everyone has the option to charge their car on the driveway. As a municipality, we want to facilitate charging points.

115386.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants to make traffic in Hattem much safer for cyclists and pedestrians, from young to old; therefore, the entire built-up area will have a 30 km/h speed limit.

115387.explanation: The position is clear. People who need care can make their own choice. This remains an independent decision, regardless of the availability of care providers.

115388.explanation: Price is not the only thing the municipality must consider. Proximity, availability (no waiting lists), quality, intended results, etc., also matter.

115389.explanation: We pay attention to a healthy municipal budget. Raising local taxes is, in our view, the last resort to balance the budget.

115392.explanation: The municipality of Hattem coordinates well with Triada (housing corporation) regarding the allocation of available homes. We continue to help refugees and status holders find a house and receive the right guidance to participate in Hattem. Therefore, more homes need to be built.

115396.explanation: As ChristenUnie, we are PROUD of our Hanseatic city Hattem. The municipality has invested in cultural education for years. Together with cultural providers, children encounter the rich history of Hattem. The Attems dialect is part of this.

115399.explanation: The new development location ’t Veen allows for 'high-rise' buildings. These are not "apartment blocks" but residential buildings with a maximum of 6 floors.

115400.explanation: This must be possible, provided the requirements of the Affordable Rent Act are met.

115401.explanation: For ChristenUnie, wind turbines are not a goal in themselves. We take responsibility for what we consume in Hattem and therefore strive to be energy neutral. We do not exclude any options for energy generation or heat sources.

115402.explanation: The current location of the town hall is not a point of discussion for ChristenUnie. It is a beautiful location central in Hattem. Like any building, the town hall requires maintenance. We must take responsibility for this.

115351.explanation: Given the small executive board of a maximum of 4 people, it is not standardly feasible for Hattem. We aim for quality in any case.

115352.explanation: A Citizens' Assembly is even fairer than a referendum. Unfortunately, organizing this is very costly. SamenHattem also provides a platform for residents.

115355.explanation: Agriculture must become more sustainable, and we want farmers to continue earning money with their businesses. A shop with good products from their own land or a nice campsite can help with that. The initiative must also fit within the environmental plan.

115356.explanation: If residents generate their own green and affordable electricity together, everyone benefits. The network is not burdened. The municipality has a stimulating and facilitating role, for example through the Green Deal or PoweredByHattem.

115357.explanation: The Netherlands already has many large distribution centers. They spoil the landscape, and much work is done by poorly paid migrant workers. Distribution centers for our region remain welcome. With this policy, we also protect our retailers.

115358.explanation: We support the Distribution Act adopted by the House of Representatives. Hattem is too small to independently host an asylum seekers' center (AZC). Regionally, we want to take our responsibility.

115360.explanation: With poor air quality, the municipality must be able to enforce regulations when residents experience nuisance from wood burning over a longer period. A complete ban is unnecessary as long as people burn properly.

115362.explanation: Setting off fireworks in one's own neighborhood was always a nice and cozy tradition. Now that safety and nuisance for people and animals are increasingly an issue, we support the national policy.

115363.explanation: We support the current policy of ROVA. If all waste collection were free, citizens would no longer be aware of the negative impact of waste on our environment.

115367.explanation: Sports provide a lot of energy and fulfillment, so it must also be accessible for people with a limited income.

115368.explanation: Local media are important to know what is happening in your own environment, from the street to the municipal council. We believe the municipality has a role here. Sponsorship from society also remains welcome.

115373.explanation: Safety in the neighborhood is important. Additional enforcement officers can contribute to visibility and approachability. This affects contact with residents, entrepreneurs, and institutions such as Welfare. Investigating officers are also needed outside office hours.

115374.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is important. However, your privacy is a great good that must not be infringed without reason. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong. Cameras like those near the Verkentoren are necessary.

115375.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable for our region.

115376.explanation: A starter subsidy encourages young buyers to take the step towards owning a home and promotes equal opportunities. However, this will cause house prices to rise and poses too great a financial risk for the municipality.

115379.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage. But as long as the need is great, some people can use them. Permanent residence would put too much pressure on our beautiful nature along the IJssel River and in the Veluwe forest.

115380.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery in and around Hattem as best as possible. Hattem is already constructing a lot of new housing within the built-up area, now also in the Het Veen neighborhood.

115381.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions actually create more uncertainty. However, socially meaningful work can provide satisfaction.

115383.explanation: The maximum legally permitted poverty alleviation already supports our party in Hattem.

115385.explanation: The availability of charging stations encourages the purchase of electric cars and is therefore better for the environment. The number of electric cars is increasing, so the number of public charging points must increase.

115386.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

115387.explanation: This statement is not relevant. 1) Every caregiver respects the care recipient 2) Politics has no influence on the beliefs of the caregivers working in Hattem.

115388.explanation: Quality takes precedence over price. And quality youth care can mean that the care only lasts a short time, making it inexpensive in the long term.

115389.explanation: The municipality has little money. We want all children in Hattem to learn to swim, have enough playgrounds, and be able to make music. We also want elderly people to continue meeting each other. Privileged Hattem residents who own a home may continue to contribute to this.

115392.explanation: About 7% of the available homes are allocated to status holders. A status holder is not an ordinary first-time home buyer. This vulnerable group would otherwise become homeless or return to emergency shelter.

115396.explanation: Schools already have many demands. No matter how proud we are of the dialect, let's especially encourage schools to have the basics in order: good reading and arithmetic education.

115399.explanation: We voted for the 't Veen plan, which includes high-rise buildings up to 6 floors. Near the nature of the IJssel, we want a maximum of 4 floors. Elsewhere in Hattem, we do not want to build that high.

115401.explanation: We strive for more sustainable energy and heating. Wind energy on Hattem's territory should be possible, provided that local residents also benefit.

115402.explanation: The Town Hall belongs in the city center, especially regarding civil registration. If space is lacking, alternative locations can be considered for other departments.

115351.explanation: We aim for proportional representation, but quality is decisive.

115352.explanation: A proper participation process must be followed for important decisions.

115357.explanation: A small-scale distribution center should be possible at H2O.

115358.explanation: Small-scale asylum reception should be possible in Hattem.

115360.explanation: We assume conscious heating. During code red, residents must take their own responsibility.

115363.explanation: You can have it collected or bring it yourself to Rova.

115367.explanation: This is in the context of special social assistance and the Leergeld foundation.

115375.explanation: We stand for a regional approach.

115376.explanation: Based on clear criteria, to be determined further.

115381.explanation: Participation in society is always good, but personal circumstances must be considered.

115383.explanation: Hattem already uses the 130% income threshold.

115385.explanation: Focus on cable trays and on private property.

115387.explanation: Where customized solutions can be provided, that is fine.

115388.explanation: Price must be proportional to quality.

115392.explanation: The municipality must fulfill its targets.

115399.explanation: In some places, building upwards can be attractive.

115351.explanation: The recent balance was three women and one man. It would be nice if the distribution is equal, but the first requirement must be whether someone is suitable for the role.

115352.explanation: There is already a referendum every four years, called the municipal elections. For decisions that directly affect citizens, such as local housing developments, we will actively engage residents in participation.

115355.explanation: VVD Hattem wants to encourage entrepreneurship, allowing room for creative solutions. Initiatives must fit within the framework of the established environmental vision.

115356.explanation: The municipality currently does not have the funds for this. However, we can assist and encourage residents, for example through advice and by abolishing fees for these initiatives.

115357.explanation: VVD Hattem commits to a good business climate. Regionally, we need to identify suitable locations for these centers, which could include H2O. Of course, this must be done sustainably and energy-efficiently.

115358.explanation: We support the dispersion law, but currently there are no facilities within the municipality to house asylum seekers. The solution lies in a regional plan and cooperation with neighboring municipalities.

115360.explanation: VVD Hattem believes residents should be free to burn wood responsibly. Therefore, only burn dry wood and no painted or impregnated wood. Also, follow the instructions of your stove or fireplace. A ban is not allowed since most wood stoves are approved on the European market.

115362.explanation: The municipality can enable associations to organize fireworks at designated locations under clear conditions.

115363.explanation: VVD Hattem has a strong preference for its own (possibly regional) environmental street.

115367.explanation: Membership improves health and encourages participation in society. It also prevents social isolation. VVD Hattem believes that people receiving social assistance benefits have the right to equal opportunities. Priority should first be given to initiatives like the Leergeld Foundation and national subsidies.

115368.explanation: VVD Hattem places great value on the local broadcaster; however, the municipality of Hattem currently does not have sufficient funds for additional financial support.

115373.explanation: Visible security increases the feeling of safety and works preventively. As VVD Hattem, we also want to introduce the green BOA (special enforcement officer). This officer can protect our natural areas, combat poaching, and actively monitor the presence of wolves.

115374.explanation: It is an extreme measure, but in places where disturbances are regularly reported, such as the Verkentoren, camera surveillance could be a solution.

115375.explanation: International agreements require each country to have at least one emergency shelter for victims of domestic violence per 10,000 inhabitants. For the Netherlands, this means 1,800 places, but currently, there are only about 1,000 shelter spots. Hattem can also play a role in the regional response.

115376.explanation: The municipality has already studied the possibility of introducing starters loans as a tool to support young home seekers in the purchase market. The results show that such loans can only help a few home seekers and that the financial risks are considerable.

115379.explanation: It should not be a permanent solution but can temporarily relieve pressure on the housing market. Each case should be assessed individually.

115380.explanation: New construction must be possible, including outside the existing city boundaries, if the initiative fits within the current environmental vision and preserves the characteristic appearance and natural values of Hattem. By the way, Hattem is a city!

115381.explanation: Those on social assistance must (if possible) return to work, participate, and contribute to society. This also applies to newcomers. However, these decisions are made nationally, not locally.

115383.explanation: We must align with national policy and not pursue our own independent course.

115385.explanation: The goal should not be more spots but enough spots in good locations. We also want to provide more options for people without their own driveway to charge at home, for example by facilitating cable conduits or similar solutions.

115386.explanation: This would mean that the road layout would need to be adjusted in many places. We believe it is more important to simplify the patchwork of rules and to implement and enforce a uniform speed policy. Dangerous spots should be addressed quickly.

115387.explanation: VVD Hattem stands for freedom and personal choice. Residents themselves know which care fits their beliefs. The municipality facilitates this where possible and otherwise engages in dialogue to arrive at appropriate and reliable care together.

115388.explanation: Quality is always the most important factor for VVD Hattem. However, research shows that more expensive youth care often performs worse. There are many unnecessary costs. The municipality must find a sensible middle ground: procure sharply and manage available funds efficiently.

115389.explanation: Nowhere has the property tax (OZB) risen as sharply as in Hattem. To balance the budget, homeowners are being treated like a cash cow. Instead of raising taxes, we first want to explore how the government can reduce costs.

115392.explanation: Hattem has a quota to house status holders. Due to a shortage of rental homes, priority must be given to status holders to meet this quota. However, VVD Hattem believes other vulnerable groups should also receive priority. We focus on customized solutions.

115396.explanation: Schools decide independently on their educational programs, and politics should not interfere. Or as an Attemer might say: "That's none of your business. Don't meddle."

115399.explanation: We recently decided that six stories is the maximum height allowed in certain places in ‘t Veen. That now seems sufficient for Hattem’s scale.

115400.explanation: We must look openly at all solutions to reduce pressure on the housing market. This certainly does not mean that everything can be subdivided into multiple rooms. Every initiative must be thoroughly assessed.

115401.explanation: New large wind turbines do not fit within the current environmental vision.

115402.explanation: The current location is not sustainable and suffers from deferred maintenance. Our civil servants deserve a sustainable, safe working environment. VVD Hattem wants to explore whether a cheaper city office outside the center is feasible, while preserving the historic front section for ceremonies.

115351.explanation: Our focus is on quality and equal opportunities, not fixed quotas. In the current college, women are even in the majority, showing that a mandatory distribution is not necessary to achieve a balanced composition.

115352.explanation: Residents should have more direct influence on important decisions. New forms of participation, such as citizen panels or consultative referenda, can help with that. For complex decisions with major consequences, the municipal council remains responsible for careful consideration.

115355.explanation: Farmers may expand their business with, for example, a shop or campsite, but not automatically. It must fit the location, surroundings, and scale of the business.

115356.explanation: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together deserve support. This can be via financial assistance or removal of barriers, provided the initiative is feasible and benefits the local community.

115357.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers do not match Hattem’s scale and character. We choose businesses that align with our environment, accessibility, and quality of life.

115358.explanation: Whether there will be an asylum seekers' center depends on the location and circumstances. We are not fundamentally opposed to shelters, but such decisions cannot be predetermined by a general obligation.

115360.explanation: Wood smoke is harmful to health and causes severe complaints. The municipality must be able to limit or discourage its use where it leads to health problems or persistent nuisance.

115362.explanation: If consumer fireworks are banned, the municipality can organize a joint fireworks show. This offers a safe alternative and limits nuisance.

115363.explanation: Bulky waste collection must be properly arranged but not always free. It costs community money, and funds should be used purposefully.

115367.explanation: Sport and exercise must be accessible to everyone. For people receiving social assistance, the municipality can help pay membership fees so that money is not a barrier to participation.

115368.explanation: Additional support may be needed when the local broadcaster performs public tasks. This requires clear agreements about quality and spending.

115373.explanation: Hattem is a small municipality with limited safety issues. With the current BOAs, enforcement is already taking place. Safety requires visibility and cooperation with the police, not automatically more BOAs.

115374.explanation: Camera surveillance is a serious invasion of privacy and does not suit Hattem. It offers a false sense of security and does not solve underlying problems.

115375.explanation: Shelters for victims of domestic violence must be well and safely arranged. This is organized regionally. Hattem is too small for its own facility but must ensure quick access to appropriate help.

115376.explanation: A starters loan can help young people in Hattem buy their first home. This carries financial risks, but these can be limited with clear conditions and a restricted budget.

115379.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. It has also been agreed in Hattem that permanent residence there is undesirable. We solve the housing shortage with real residential construction, not through recreational parks.

115380.explanation: To build sufficient and affordable homes, expansion is necessary. New construction outside the current built-up area must be possible, provided it is carefully integrated and takes landscape and living environment into account.

115381.explanation: Participation is important, but a general mandatory counter-performance does not suit everyone. The municipality must deliver tailored support and encourage participation or work, not enforce employment.

115383.explanation: Many residents with a low or just-above-low income currently fall outside support schemes, even though they struggle to make ends meet. By raising the income threshold, support reaches this group as well and prevents bigger problems.

115385.explanation: Electric driving requires sufficient charging options. By designating more parking spaces as charging spots, the transition to cleaner transportation becomes achievable for more residents.

115386.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases traffic safety and livability in residential streets. It makes the city safer for pedestrians and cyclists and reduces noise and accidents.

115387.explanation: Good care takes into account who a person is. Wherever possible, people should be able to choose a care provider that fits their faith, culture, or belief system, within the limits of availability and quality.

115388.explanation: In youth care, quality and continuity should be prioritized. Overemphasis on price often comes at the expense of appropriate help and frequently leads to higher long-term costs.

115389.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is necessary to pay for facilities and investments. Lowering it sounds attractive but often leads to cuts elsewhere or poorer services. We choose stable expenses and good governance.

115392.explanation: Municipalities have a legal duty to house status holders. This requires careful balancing within the available housing stock, with attention to urgency and other housing seekers.

115396.explanation: Schools decide for themselves how to organize their education. Hattems is part of the local heritage, but education is primarily focused on proper Dutch. Attention to the regional language can be supplementary and voluntary, not mandatory.

115399.explanation: High residential towers do not fit the scale and character of Hattem. Housing construction is needed but must align with the existing environment, for example in the form of low- to mid-rise buildings that are carefully integrated.

115400.explanation: Unlimited renting by the room can lead to nuisance and damage the livability of neighborhoods. The municipality must promote balanced housing types and prevent residential areas from coming under pressure.

115401.explanation: Wind turbines north of the A28 can contribute to the energy transition, especially if residents and local parties are co-owners. Local ownership ensures that proceeds flow back to the community and increases support.

115402.explanation: The town hall should be visibly located and accessible in the heart of the city. This strengthens the bond between residents and government and keeps the center lively. Moving outside the center does not offer clear added value.

115351.explanation: Aldermen should be appointed based on expertise, local knowledge, and involvement.

115352.explanation: Only by exception. Only on very important issues with significant impact for the entire community. A citizen council can be a better solution.

115355.explanation: Depending on spatial integration and potential nuisance. It is good to offer farmers more perspective.

115356.explanation: The municipality’s primary task here is to create the right preconditions.

115357.explanation: There is insufficient space in our municipality for such a large block structure.

115358.explanation: The municipality must comply with laws, and therefore we must implement the Distribution Act. But it must be appropriate to the size of the Hattem community.

115360.explanation: Maintain current policy. So provide good information about the impact of wood burning on the environment and encourage people to use the Heating Guide.

115362.explanation: The municipality can play a facilitating role.

115363.explanation: Bulky waste is not a problem in our municipality.

115374.explanation: In exceptional cases, if the issue cannot be addressed otherwise, such as with the Verkentoren.

115375.explanation: This is provided by the central municipality.

115376.explanation: However, Hattem residents should be able to have priority when buying a house.

115379.explanation: Holiday homes should remain available for recreation and tourism.

115380.explanation: In case housing construction on infill locations stagnates, the municipality must have an alternative. It must comply with spatial regulations.

115381.explanation: People on social assistance should be supported as well as possible to return to work.

115385.explanation: If there is a need, a good location should be sought in consultation with the neighborhood.

115386.explanation: Most of the built-up area is already a 30 km/h zone. Where 50 km/h still applies, it should be reconsidered, for example on Verlengde Parklaan.

115387.explanation: This is in the spirit of the constitution.

115388.explanation: Quality should also be a criterion.

115389.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has been increased far too much in recent years. The financial problems should not be borne solely by the citizens.

115396.explanation: As part of broader attention for the local living environment and local culture.

115399.explanation: High-rise buildings do not fit the character of Hattem.

115400.explanation: This is already possible in Hattem under certain conditions.

115401.explanation: This has been confirmed several times by the municipal council, in the Environmental Vision and the Rural Zoning Plan. There is no reason to change that position.

115402.explanation: Preserve the front part of the town hall for ceremonial occasions and council meetings. The civil servants could be housed at De Marke. The rear part can be sold for apartments.

statements.title: Wind turbines should be installed north of the A28.

titleParts.text: Ten noorden van de A28 moeten

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: Residents of Hattem should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hattem moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Hattem.

titleParts.text: In Hattem moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Hattem should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Camera surveillance should be introduced in Hattem.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
A women's shelter should be established in Hattem.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include education about the Hattem dialect.
High-rise apartment buildings should be permitted in Hattem.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The town hall should move to a location outside the town center.
